title: Add build information for Ubuntu
body:
We should document requied packages for installing on Ubuntu and Debian
to match up with the use on Fedora.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: This fixes https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/issues/243
@alexellis PTAL and verify that these commands would work for you on Ubuntu.
@alexellis @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL and see if these commands work on Ubuntu

I'll try building this in a trusty container (so it's a repeatable build) and get back to you.

Could probably use `--no-install-recommends` too.

https://gist.github.com/alexellis/51e83db74ca18c6c07e00fafc766a9b3

Failure pulling in deps:

```
 ---> Running in e3c5e3c94d8b
Reading package lists...
Building dependency tree...
Reading state information...
E: Unable to locate package bats
E: Unable to locate package libostree-dev
The command '/bin/sh -c apt-get -qy install bats btrfs-tools git libapparmor-dev libdevmapper-dev libglib2.0-dev libgpgme11-dev libostree-dev libseccomp-dev libselinux1-dev' returned a non-zero code: 100
```

Forcing an apt update just before gets us slightly further forward:

```
E: Unable to locate package libostree-dev
```

add-apt-repository ppa:alexlarsson/flatpak 
is supposed to make that available?

If you build with TAGS="containers_image_ostree_stub apparmor seccomp"
It should build without using the libostree.  And buildah really does not need that.

@alexellis The [flatpak PPA](https://launchpad.net/~alexlarsson/+archive/ubuntu/flatpak) only contains `ostree` builds for xenial and later.  IIRC trusty's version of `libseccomp` is too old for the version of the Go bindings for it that we end up pulling in.

I tried a new gist with Xenial as the base image and got a bit further, but there's still things missing.

```
make[1]: Entering directory '/root/go/src/github.com/projectatomic/buildah/docs'
go-md2man -in buildah.md -out buildah.1
Makefile:9: recipe for target 'buildah.1' failed
make[1]: go-md2man: Command not found
make[1]: *** [buildah.1] Error 127
make: *** [docs] Error 2
```

Well that is a lot further.
Do make install.tools
And that should get you go-md2man.
`make install.tools` didn't work.. perhaps one of you guys could get this to a working state from the gist? I don't know the dependencies well enough and we're just playing GitHub 🎾

```
Step 15/17 : RUN PATH=/usr/lib/go-1.8/bin:$PATH make install.tools && make runc all TAGS="apparmor seccomp"
 ---> Running in e5b9f6a72c56
go get -u -tags " libdm_no_deferred_remove seccomp" github.com/cpuguy83/go-md2man
go get -u -tags " libdm_no_deferred_remove seccomp" github.com/vbatts/git-validation
go get -u -tags " libdm_no_deferred_remove seccomp" gopkg.in/alecthomas/gometalinter.v1
gometalinter.v1 -i
make: gometalinter.v1: Command not found
make: *** [install.tools] Error 127
Makefile:53: recipe for target 'install.tools' failed
The command '/bin/sh -c PATH=/usr/lib/go-1.8/bin:$PATH make install.tools && make runc all TAGS="apparmor seccomp"' returned a non-zero code: 2
```

I would love to start playing with this... possibly to do builds for [OpenFaaS](https://github.com/alexellis/faas-cli). Please could someone help us come to a working Docker build?

I managed to get a Docker image built, but when I use it, I get an error about overlay:

```
ERRO[0000] 'overlay' is not supported over overlay      
ERRO[0000] 'overlay' is not supported over overlay      
ERRO[0000] 'overlay' is not supported over overlay: backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver
'overlay' is not supported over overlay: backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver 
```

I also tried bind-mounting a local ext4 path into a container.
You have to volume mount in /var/lib/containers into your container, otherwize you are doing Overlay over Overlay.
@lsm5 PTAL

@TomSweeneyRedHat Were you able to get any further with the invalid memory issue on Ubuntu?
@nalind Any ideas on this?

@rhatdan I've not gotten closer to finding the issue.  I pinged lsm5 to talk it through and we discovered that the kit he builds in ppa is also having troubles.  I've been testing his newer spins while he works through that.  Once that works I hope to see what he did to build the kit and see if we can use that here.

running into some build issues trying to bump buildah to 0.3 on zesty and
xenial. Let me get back in a bit

On Fri, Aug 25, 2017 at 01:49:58PM +0000, Tom Sweeney wrote:
> TomSweeneyRedHat commented on this pull request.

> 
> 
> > +  apt-get install software-properties-common
> +  add-apt-repository ppa:duggan/bats
> +  add-apt-repository ppa:alexlarsson/flatpak
> +  add-apt-repository ppa:gophers/archive
> +  apt-get install bats btrfs-tools git libapparmor-dev libdevmapper-dev libglib2.0-dev libgpgme11-dev libostree-dev libseccomp-dev libselinux1-dev
> +  apt-get install golang-1.8
> +```
> +Then to install buildah on Ubuntu/Debian follow the steps in this example:
> +
> +```
> +  mkdir ~/buildah
> +  cd ~/buildah
> +  export GOPATH=`pwd`
> +  git clone https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah ./src/github.com/projectatomic/buildah
> +  cd ./src/github.com/projectatomic/buildah
> +  PATH=/usr/lib/go-1.8/bin:$PATH make runc all TAGS="apparmor seccomp"
> 
> ok, maybe it's an ubuntu thing, haven't run into this on other platforms.
>

Is it possible to build images within a Docker image using an unprivileged `buildah` container?

@alexellis Depends on what you define as "unprivileged",  buildah will require the SYS_ADMIN privilege because it needs to mount file systems.  This would also cause a looser SELinux policy to be added, something we are investigating.  Finally /var/lib/containers/storage would need to be mounted into the container.

Other confinement would still be allowed.

@TomSweeneyRedHat I've updated buildah to the latest master branch on zesty and
xenial in the PPA.

If the PPA deb gets installed and works fine for you, that'll give me a better
picture on this PR.

On Tue, Aug 29, 2017 at 03:26:27AM -0700, Daniel J Walsh wrote:
> @alexellis Depends on what you define as "unprivileged",  buildah will require the SYS_ADMIN privilege because it needs to mount file systems.  This would also cause a looser SELinux policy to be added, something we are investigating.  Finally /var/lib/containers/storage would need to be mounted into the container.
> 
> Other confinement would still be allowed.

>    buildah --help
>  ```
>  
> +In Ubuntu/Debian, you can use this command:
> +
> +```
> +  apt-get -y install software-properties-common
> +  add-apt-repository -y ppa:duggan/bats
> +  add-apt-repository -y ppa:alexlarsson/flatpak
> +  add-apt-repository -y ppa:gophers/archive
> +  apt-add-repository -y ppa:projectatomic/ppa
> +  apt-get -y -qq update
> 
> Just tested on a UBUNTU server on my work pc and Buildah was up and running afterwards, but this update line threw up a bit.  @lsm5, any thoughts?  I think overall it LGTM and we should ship now and tweak as necessary later on.
> 
> root@ubuntu:~# apt-get -y -qq update
> W: The repository 'http://ppa.launchpad.net/duggan/bats/ubuntu zesty Release' does not have a Release file.
> E: Failed to fetch http://ppa.launchpad.net/duggan/bats/ubuntu/dists/zesty/main/binary-i386/Packages  404  Not Found
> E: Some index files failed to download. They have been ignored, or old ones used instead.

That's because there's nothing for zesty yet in that ppa.
See: http://ppa.launchpad.net/duggan/bats/ubuntu/dists/

What package do you need that for?

@rh-atomic-bot r+ 1c8545f
:hourglass: Testing commit 1c8545f with merge 999748c...

@lsm5 I just tested and did not include this step:

 add-apt-repository -y ppa:duggan/bats

Everything built fine and I did not get any of the errors I was seeing earlier.  

If the bot merges this automatically, I'll go back and remove that line in a follow up PR., I don't think it's necessary.
:boom: Test timed out
Removed bats line.
@rh-atomic-bot retest
@rh-atomic-bot retest
@cevich PTAL

```
### Wed Sep  6 10:32:08 UTC 2017
### PR #244
### BUILD_ID 13657
...
BDB1539 Build signature doesn't match environment failed loading RPMDB
```

That appears exceptionally bad to me, did the VM run out of disk space or somesuch?

I really have no idea TBH, probably need to ask @jlebon if he has any guesses 😖

Yeah, this is a known issue right now with the Fedora 26 Docker image: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1483553.

For examples on how to work around it temporarily, see:

https://github.com/ostreedev/ostree/pull/1143
https://github.com/projectatomic/rpm-ostree/pull/975
Thx Jon, there's your answer @rhatdan 😀
FWIW, I vote to merge this as is.  There's no code changes, only documentation and the build issue is completely unrelated.
Merging since the tests are unrelated.
